title_en: The World Bank has ditched its climate targets
title_zh: 世界银行已经抛弃了其气候目标
rubric_en: The rise and fall of the institution’s love for green growth
rubric_zh: 这个机构对绿色增长热情的兴衰

paragraphs.en: IN JUNE 2023 the world’s biggest development bank seemed to be undergoing a transformation. At a summit in Paris, the president of the World Bank, which for 70 years had focused only on tackling global poverty, announced plans to reserve a large portion of lending for projects to alleviate climate change and its effects.
paragraphs.zh: 2023年6月，世界上最大的开发性银行看起来正在发生转型。在巴黎的一次峰会上，运行了70年的世界银行行长宣布计划拿出贷款的大部分用于缓解气候变化及其影响的项目。

paragraphs.en: The bank’s then newish boss, Ajay Banga, had been hired in part for his green credentials, having pledged to plant 100m trees in his previous job as the chief executive of Mastercard, and he was proving popular. The crowd, which included Emmanuel Macron, the French president, and Abiy Ahmed, the prime minister of Ethiopia, celebrated with a standing ovation. As Mr Banga left the stage, he was diverted by Mr Abiy’s aide for a selfie. A year later the World Bank ponied up $43bn, or 44% of the year’s lending, nearly hitting its formal target of channelling 45% of its loans to such causes.
paragraphs.zh: 当时的新上任行长阿贾伊·班加（Ajay Banga）部分得益于其绿色履历而被聘任；他在担任万事达（Mastercard）首席执行官时曾承诺种植1亿棵树，并且很受欢迎。包括法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙和埃塞俄比亚总理阿比·艾哈迈德在内的与会者报以起立鼓掌。班加先生离开讲台时，还被艾比的助理拦下要求合影。就在一年后，世界银行注资430亿美元，占当年贷款总额的44%，几乎触及其正式提出的45%贷款投向这类领域的目标。

paragraphs.en: Now the party is over. Last month, under American pressure, the 45% target was formally ditched. Bank officials have barely mentioned climate since Donald Trump returned to office in early 2025 (Mr Trump thinks climate change is a hoax and wants more fossil-fuel use, not less). European countries, with more environmentally minded electorates, fought hard for the target to stay.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，庆祝已经结束。上个月，世界银行在美国压力下正式取消了45%的目标。自唐纳德·特朗普2025年初再次就任以来，银行官员几乎未再提气候问题（特朗普认为气候变化是骗局，并希望更多地使用化石燃料，而不是更少）。拥有更环保选民基础的欧洲国家则曾拼命争取保留该目标。

paragraphs.en: The sudden drought in climate finance could, it is true, slow the developing world’s decarbonisation efforts. However, for the world’s poorest countries, the World Bank’s climate reversal could yet turn out to be good news.
paragraphs.zh: 这种气候金融的突然收缩，确实可能会放慢发展中国家去碳化进程。然而对世界上最贫困国家来说，世界银行的气候立场转变最终也可能带来好消息。

paragraphs.en: The green shift was always controversial. A split soon emerged between development types, inside and outside the bank. One group believed that spending on things like wind turbines (for renewable power), electric vehicles (to reduce pollution) and mangrove forests (to stop flooding) were vital to prepare developing countries for rising temperatures. Another lot believed that this diverted money from clinics, schools and roads that immediately helped the poorest places.
paragraphs.zh: “绿色转型”一直都很有争议。很快，银行内外出现了意见分歧。一个群体认为，在风机（用于可再生发电）、电动汽车（用于减少污染）和红树林（用于防止洪水）等领域投入资金，对帮助发展中国家应对气温上升至关重要。另一个群体则认为，这会把钱从立即惠及最贫困地区、如诊所、学校和道路建设的项目中挪走。

paragraphs.en: To sharpen the division, Mr Banga’s big climate push happened just when rich countries were falling out of love with development assistance. As aid budgets and contributions to the World Bank froze in real terms, climate commitments would require budget cuts elsewhere. Bank officials demurred, saying that projects that alleviated poverty also protected the planet. Mr Banga argued that there was little difference between spending on one or the other. If that were true, many officials in the poorest countries wondered, why ring-fence climate spending at all?
paragraphs.zh: 为了加剧这种分裂，Banga先生的大规模气候推动正值富裕国家对发展援助逐渐失去热情之际。随着援助预算和对世界银行的出资在按实际价值计算上停滞不前，气候承诺就意味着必须在其他领域削减预算。银行官员辩解称，缓解贫困的项目同样有助于保护地球。Banga先生则认为，把钱花在这两类项目上几乎没有区别。如果这是真的，许多最贫困国家的官员就会想：既然如此，为什么还要单独设立气候支出专用资金？

paragraphs.en: At the end of 2024 the bank increased its climate pledges again, promising to provide $120bn annually by 2030, along with other international institutions. However, Mr Trump’s return to the White House stopped the momentum. America’s concerns ran deeper than the president’s reflexive dislike for green do-goodery. In October 2025 Scott Bessent, the treasury secretary, proclaimed that focusing on climate was distracting from helping poor countries grow. He hinted that the bank needed to pull back or lose American support—a stark choice, since America is its biggest shareholder.
paragraphs.zh: 到2024年底，银行再次提高了其气候承诺，承诺与其他国际机构一道到2030年每年提供1200亿美元。然而，特朗普先生重返白宫后，势头被打断。美国的担忧比特朗普对“绿色善举”的本能反感更为根深蒂固。2025年10月，财政部长斯科特·贝森特宣称，把重点放在气候上会分散帮助穷国发展的努力。他暗示银行需要收缩，否则将失去美国支持——这是一个严峻选择，因为美国是其最大股东。

paragraphs.en: By April Mr Bessent’s officials had begun a quiet campaign to scrap the targets. Little that Mr Banga did to appease Mr Trump helped, including joining the Board of Peace, the committee supposed to oversee the reconstruction of Gaza. The protests of many European countries were weakened when war in Iran was making net-zero commitments that raise the price of petrol less popular at home.
paragraphs.zh: 到了4月，贝森特的官员已经开始发起一场悄然取消目标的运动。Banga先生为安抚特朗普所做的一切都没起作用，其中包括加入负责加沙重建的“和平委员会”。当伊朗战争升温时，许多欧洲国家的抗议也被削弱，因为在本国那样抬高汽油价格的“净零排放”承诺并不受欢迎。

paragraphs.en: The question now is what happens to the balance of the bank’s lending. The climate push meant more aid ended up with middle-income countries, which pollute more than the poorest and therefore find decarbonising costlier. So policymakers in the world’s poorest countries are relieved at the prospect that the needle swings back.
paragraphs.zh: 现在的问题是世界银行的放贷平衡将何去何从。气候推进意味着更多援助最终流向中等收入国家，而中等收入国家的污染程度高于最贫穷国家，因此它们实现脱碳的成本更高。因此，世界上最贫困国家的政策制定者对资金重新回流到中低收入国家的前景感到欣慰。

paragraphs.en: Yet some at the World Bank think that ditching climate targets will make no difference to the institution’s portfolio. New climate projects could simply be repackaged and approved as development loans. If so, many staff will consider that to be a victory stolen from a meddlesome America. At the same time, however, it may also show that the sceptical development types were right—and there was little need for targets to start with. ■
paragraphs.zh: 但世界银行的一些人认为，放弃气候目标并不会改变该机构的投资组合。新的气候项目可以简单地改头换面，重新包装为发展贷款并获批准。如果真是如此，许多工作人员会把这当作从事事不决、爱插手的美国那里‘赢来’的一场胜利。与此同时，这也可能表明那些对发展立场持怀疑态度的人是对的——一开始其实根本不需要这些目标。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取更多关于经济、金融和市场重大新闻的专家解读，请订阅《Money Talks》——我们每周面向订阅者的通讯。
